title: Antirhea granulata
intro: Antirhea granulata, a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in the treatment of wounds and infections. The bioactive constituents of Antirhea granulata include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Antirhea granulata is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the form of a tincture or extract to leverage its bioactive constituents for therapeutic purposes.

This family, known as the madder family, includes a wide range of species, many of which are found in tropical and subtropical regions.
